Front: How do you promote a new model to production with the MLflow registry, without changing code?
Back: Register the new model as a new version (v2), then move the alias (e.g. @champion) to v2 - in the UI or via set_registered_model_alias. Restart the app (model loads at startup), and it now serves v2. No edit to train.py or main.py, no redeploy. The alias is the switch; code references the stable alias.

Front: What does models:/iris-clf@champion resolve to, and why is that indirection valuable?
Back: At load time it resolves to the concrete model version currently holding the @champion alias, which points to that version's stored artifact. The code references a stable symbolic name; the alias->version->artifact mapping is registry data changeable independent of code (like a DNS hostname pointing at an IP). That decoupling lets ops promote/rollback without touching code.

Front: Rolling update vs self-healing: how many ReplicaSets, and what is the '3'?
Back: Rolling update (image/spec change): TWO ReplicaSets - old (v1) + new (v2); new scales its pods up while old scales its pods down. Self-heal (a pod crashes): ONE ReplicaSet recreates the missing pod. The '3' is the number of PODS (replicas) a ReplicaSet manages, not the number of ReplicaSets. ReplicaSet = the controller; replica/pod = what it counts.

Front: Name the four Docker restart policies.
Back: no (never restart, default), on-failure (restart only on a non-zero/error exit; a clean finish stays down), always (restart no matter what, incl. daemon restart), unless-stopped (like always but a manual stop sticks - respects operator intent). Purpose: automatic crash recovery.

Front: MLflow serving: what is MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI, and what changes to load from the registry inside a K8s pod?
Back: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI is the ADDRESS of the MLflow tracking/registry server (WHERE to fetch from); the alias is WHICH model. The app code is unchanged in a pod, but localhost won't reach your laptop - so run MLflow as a Service, inject the URI via a ConfigMap using K8s DNS (e.g. http://mlflow:5000), and use a shared artifact store (S3/GCS/MinIO) the pod can reach.
